action.reasoning: Observation: checkpoint inspection shows keys: t_enc, q_enc, blocks.0-5 (each with conv1, bn1, conv2, bn2, skip (Conv1d)), t_head, q_head, scalar_fc.0, scalar_fc.3. No scalar_fc.2, indicating Dropout layer at index 2. The ResBlock always uses a Conv1d skip even when channels match. The scalar_fc sequential likely Linear→ReLU→Dropout→Linear. Analysis: To load the checkpoint successfully and perform eval, must exactly match this architecture. The post-processing overwriting humidity tendencies with training mean should dramatically improve mean_r2 (from ~-1e7 to ~0.27+). Decision: rewrite model.py with corrected ClimateResCNN (ResBlock with Conv1d skip, scalar_fc with Dropout, 4-layer sequential). Update predict.py to use get_model generically from config. Verify loading with a quick test, then submit eval. No training needed.
action.command: python -c "import torch; from model import get_model; checkpoint = torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False); cfg = checkpoint['model_config']; model = get_model(cfg['name'], **{k:v for k,v in cfg.items() if k!='name'}); model.load_state_dict(checkpoint['model_state_dict']); print('Model OK, params:', sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters()))"
